prerequisites: A Mailtrap account (sign up at mailtrap.io).
At least one API token created in the Mailtrap UI, since the first token must be created manually before it can be used.
Admin permissions on the relevant Mailtrap domain if the token is used to send emails, and the appropriate access level checkboxes selected for the resources you intend to read.

authMethods.name: API token
authMethods.summary: Mailtrap authenticates requests with an API token sent in the Api-Token HTTP header (a Bearer token alternative is also supported). Provide this token in the connector's api_token field. Keep it secret and never expose it in client-side code.

setupSteps.title: Sign in to Mailtrap
setupSteps.body: Log in to your Mailtrap account at mailtrap.io. If you do not have one, create an account first.

setupSteps.title: Open API Tokens settings
setupSteps.body: In the left menu, go to Settings, then select API Tokens to view all active tokens, their creators, and their access levels.

setupSteps.title: Create a new API token
setupSteps.body: Click the Add Token button in the upper-right corner, enter a name for your reference, check the boxes for the access levels you need, then click Save to generate the token. The full token value is shown only at creation or reset and cannot be retrieved again, so copy and store it securely. Note that admin permissions on a domain are required for tokens used to send emails.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the token into the connector's required api_token field. Mailtrap will use it in the Api-Token header to authenticate API requests over HTTPS.

sources.title: Stats API (Api-Token header) - Mailtrap Help Center
